coverage.maker: partial (Vat + Dai module, DAI module 404 on original URL)
coverage.morpho: partial (index only, detailed pages behind 403/landing pages)

trust_boundaries.description: Decentralized non-custodial system; Aave Labs does not control or operate any deployed instance

invariants.over_collateralization: Borrowers must provide collateral that exceeds the borrowed amount
invariants.note: Specific LTV ratios, liquidation thresholds, and formulas not present in overview page

economic_assumptions.dual_sided_market: Suppliers provide liquidity and earn interest; borrowers pay interest on over-collateralized positions
economic_assumptions.bad_debt_coverage: Umbrella mechanism provides native coverage for bad debt
economic_assumptions.flash_loans: Available as a feature -- historically a vector for oracle manipulation and reentrancy

access_control.access_control_manager: Dedicated Access Control Manager smart contract (V3/V4)
access_control.details: Specific role/permission matrix not present in overview

oracle_dependencies.oracle_contract: Dedicated Oracles module as a smart contract dependency
oracle_dependencies.details: Oracle update frequencies, circuit breakers, and fallback mechanisms not detailed in overview

security_infrastructure.v4_new_surface: Liquidity Model with Hubs and Spokes expands cross-chain attack surface

trust_boundaries.core_vs_periphery: Core contracts handle pool logic, swaps, liquidity accounting; Periphery contracts provide user-facing helpers
trust_boundaries.factories: Factory contract deploys pools -- trust boundary between factory and individual pools

invariants.concentrated_liquidity: Liquidity providers can concentrate capital within custom price ranges (positions)
invariants.fund_conservation: For exact output swaps, amountInMaximum - amountIn must be refunded to caller
invariants.router_approval_reset: After refunding excess tokens, router approval set to zero
invariants.no_residual_balances: Contract design assumes zero token balances after each transaction
invariants.constant_product: x * y = k within active liquidity range (per whitepaper)

economic_assumptions.fixed_pool_fee_example: Single swap example uses fixed 3000 (0.3%) fee tier
economic_assumptions.protocol_fee: Protocol fee mechanism exists as a privileged administrative function

access_control.core_pools: No access control modifiers on swap functions -- any address can call
access_control.immutable_router: swapRouter set as immutable in constructor -- cannot be changed post-deployment
access_control.hardcoded_parameters: Token addresses and poolFee are constants, reducing injection attack surface
access_control.factory_owner: Factory owner can enable protocol fee and set fee amount

oracle_dependencies.built_in_twap: Protocol-level TWAP (Time-Weighted Average Price) oracle built into each pool
oracle_dependencies.no_external_oracle_in_example: Example swap contract has NO on-chain oracle integration
oracle_dependencies.critical_warning: Production deployments MUST use an oracle for amountOutMinimum -- without it, trades can be frontrun for considerable loss

security_considerations.sandwich_attacks: Setting amountOutMinimum to 0 makes swaps fully vulnerable to sandwich attacks
security_considerations.deadline_protection: Example uses block.timestamp as deadline, providing essentially zero deadline protection
security_considerations.sqrtPriceLimitX96: Set to 0 (inactive) in example -- no price boundary protection
security_considerations.unsupported_tokens: Not all ERC-20 tokens are compatible (fee-on-transfer, rebasing tokens cause issues)
security_considerations.mev_vulnerability: Smart contract swaps require external price source to prevent frontrunning
security_considerations.no_reentrancy_guards: Simplified example has no explicit reentrancy guards (low risk due to design)
security_considerations.two_step_approval: Users must approve contract, then contract approves router -- separate approval to router required

trust_boundaries.vat_is_constitution: The Vat has no external dependencies and operates as an immutable rule set -- described as 'the constitution of dss'
trust_boundaries.authorized_modules: Any module authed against the Vat has full root access and can steal all collateral
trust_boundaries.join_contracts: DaiJoin bridges internal Vat accounting to external ERC20 DAI token
trust_boundaries.adapter_boundary: Vat relies on external Adapter contracts to map collateral balances to real external balances

invariants.debt_sum: debt equals the sum of all issued dai
invariants.vice_sum: vice equals the sum of all sin (system debt)
invariants.debt_formula: debt = vice + sum(Ilk.Art * Ilk.rate) across all ilks
invariants.collateral_backing: DAI cannot exist without collateral -- every unit of DAI is backed by locked collateral
invariants.normalized_debt: Vault debt tracked as normalized art scaled by debt multiplier rate

economic_assumptions.dai_peg: DAI designed to represent any token the core system considers equal in value to its internal debt unit (1:1 peg)
economic_assumptions.over_collateralization: Vaults must maintain collateral exceeding debt value
economic_assumptions.rate_multiplier: Ilk.rate is a signed integer that can increase or decrease, affecting surplus accounting
economic_assumptions.fold_mechanism: fold modifies debt multiplier and can create or destroy DAI proportional to Ilk.Art * rate

access_control.hope_nope: Controls whether one address can modify another's gem or dai balances (wish check)
access_control.dai_transfer_rule: dai can only move with the consent of its owner (enforced via move)
access_control.gem_transfer_rule: gem can always be transferred to any address by its owner (no consent needed)
access_control.grab_authority: grab used for liquidations -- transfers collateral out and creates sin
access_control.vow_exclusive: Only the Vow holds sin; only the Vow can call heal to cancel sin against dai
access_control.slip_suck_privilege: Authorized modules can call slip (collateral modification) and suck (debt creation)

oracle_dependencies.trusted_oracles: Vat relies on trusted oracles to provide price data
oracle_dependencies.ilk_spot: Price data flows into Ilk.spot (collateral price with safety margin)
oracle_dependencies.spot_controls_draw: Ilk.spot controls how much DAI can be drawn per unit of collateral
oracle_dependencies.failure_modes: Oracle feed failures can enable minting unbacked DAI or unfair liquidation of safe Vaults

security_considerations.root_access_risk: Any authed module has full root access -- adding new modules carries systemic risk
security_considerations.erc20_race_condition: DAI susceptible to known ERC20 race condition on approvals
security_considerations.phishing_risk: Malicious join contracts could redirect tokens instead of sending to Vat
security_considerations.upgrade_risk: Vat upgrades may require new join contracts; gem token freezes can prevent collateral redemption
security_considerations.governance_risk: If cryptoeconomic protections fail, governance can authorize malicious modules
security_considerations.catastrophic_failure: Vat bug could cause loss/locking of all DAI and collateral, break auctions, prevent shutdown
security_considerations.adapter_risk: Faulty adapter could cause total collateral loss by misrepresenting external balances

trust_boundaries.oracle_trust: Protocol fundamentally trusts AccountingOracle for accurate Beacon Chain data (off-chain, cannot be verified on-chain)
trust_boundaries.dsm_trust: Deposit frontrunning protection depends entirely on DepositSecurityModule integrity
trust_boundaries.staking_router_trust: Core contract trusts StakingRouter to properly manage staking module distribution
trust_boundaries.vaulthub_trust: V3 external shares managed by VaultHub -- new trust surface
trust_boundaries.accounting_chain: AccountingOracle -> Accounting -> Lido caller hierarchy is strict

invariants.total_pooled_composition: totalPooledEther = buffered balance + transient balance + beacon balance
invariants.external_ratio_cap: getMaxExternalRatioBP() caps external shares as ratio of total shares
invariants.external_solvency: External shares tracked separately to keep core pool solvent

economic_assumptions.rebase_mechanism: Balances update algorithmically on oracle reports based on staking rewards or slashing penalties
economic_assumptions.no_transfer_events_on_rebase: No Transfer events during rebases (would require unbounded loop over all holders)
economic_assumptions.staking_rate_limit: Token bucket algorithm: max limit with per-block growth rate
economic_assumptions.external_shares_overcollateralization: stVaults implies overcollateralization requirement for external shares

access_control.PAUSE_ROLE: Controls stop() -- halts all pool operations
access_control.STAKING_PAUSE_ROLE: Controls pauseStaking() -- blocks new deposits only
access_control.UNSAFE_CHANGE_DEPOSITED_VALIDATORS_ROLE: Can manually adjust deposited validator counter (dangerous)
access_control.DepositSecurityModule: Only entity that can call deposit()
access_control.LidoExecutionLayerRewardsVault: Only entity that can call receiveELRewards()
access_control.WithdrawalVault: Only entity that can call receiveWithdrawals()

oracle_dependencies.AccountingOracle: Submits beacon chain validator counts, balances, vault balances, burner shares, withdrawal finalization
oracle_dependencies.OracleReportSanityChecker: Validates report parameters and mitigates front-running risks on rebases
oracle_dependencies.post_report_sanity: Share rate sanity check occurs in step 9 of oracle report processing
oracle_dependencies.manipulation_risk: Predictable rebases can be sandwiched -- sanity checker limits magnitude to reduce profitability

security_considerations.rebase_frontrunning: Known MEV vector; OracleReportSanityChecker limits single rebase magnitude to reduce sandwich profitability
security_considerations.erc20_non_compliance: Transfer events only emitted on explicit transfers, not during rebases -- breaks downstream contract assumptions
security_considerations.deposit_frontrunning: DepositSecurityModule exists specifically to guard against deposit frontrunning vulnerability
security_considerations.buffer_risk: User funds sit in buffer between submission and deposit -- uncommitted capital subject to liquidity risk
security_considerations.transfer_to_vault_disabled: transferToVault() always reverts -- Aragon default recovery mechanism disabled
security_considerations.unsafe_change_warning: unsafeChangeDepositedValidators() might break internal protocol state if applied incorrectly
security_considerations.pausability: Global stop halts all operations; staking pause only prevents new submissions while allowing withdrawals
security_considerations.permit_signature_support: ERC-2612 and ERC-1271 support expands attack surface for signature vulnerabilities (replay, malleability)
security_considerations.deposit_constraints: setStakingLimit reverts if maxStakeLimit is 0, >= 2^96, or if maxStakeLimit/stakeLimitIncreasePerBlock >= 2^32
security_considerations.transfer_restrictions: Recipients cannot be zero address or stETH contract itself; all operations revert when paused

eigenlayer.name: EigenLayer
eigenlayer.status: UNAVAILABLE -- docs.eigencloud.xyz returns HTTP 403 Forbidden on all endpoints

known_from_public_research.core_concept: Restaking allows ETH stakers to opt-in to validate additional services (Actively Validated Services / AVS)
known_from_public_research.slashing: AVS operators can be slashed for misbehavior; slashing conditions defined per-AVS
known_from_public_research.delegation: Stakers delegate to operators who run AVS infrastructure
known_from_public_research.trust_assumption: Stakers trust both the EigenLayer core contracts AND the AVS operators they delegate to
known_from_public_research.risk_compounding: Restaking compounds risk -- a single operator failure can affect multiple AVS simultaneously

morpho.name: Morpho

vault_roles.curators: Manage vault strategy, market selection, liquidity allocation

trust_boundaries.curator_security: Dedicated security considerations page for vault curators -- critical trust boundary
trust_boundaries.isolated_markets: Each market is self-contained; risk in one market does not directly infect another

invariants.health_factor: Health factor calculations govern liquidation eligibility
invariants.ltv_ratios: Loan-to-Value ratios as core invariants governing collateralization
invariants.solvency_enforcement: Protocol relies on collateralization enforcement to maintain solvency

economic_assumptions.adaptive_curve_irm: AdaptiveCurveIRM adjusts interest rates algorithmically -- fundamental economic invariant
economic_assumptions.isolated_market_design: Risk isolation between markets is a core architectural choice

access_control.roles_and_capabilities: Curator, allocator, and admin roles with distinct permissions
access_control.security_best_practices: Dedocumented security best practices for vault curators

oracle_dependencies.oracle_page: Dedicated Oracle page documents how oracles provide price data for Morpho markets
oracle_dependencies.manipulation_risk: Price feed manipulation or failure directly impacts liquidation thresholds and collateralization enforcement

security_considerations.flash_loans: Native flash loan feature introduces potential attack surface with oracle manipulation or reentrancy
security_considerations.audits_and_formal_verification: Protocol maintains audits page with both security audits and formal verification reports
security_considerations.contract_addresses_published: All deployed contract addresses published for independent verification
security_considerations.rewards_external_dependency: Rewards distribution via Merkl introduces external dependency for incentive delivery
security_considerations.curator_documentation: Dedicated security considerations for vault curators documented

morpho.note: Detailed contract-level security analysis requires fetching deeper documentation pages that returned 403 or were landing-only pages

cross_protocol_patterns.oracle_dependency: All protocols except Uniswap V3 core (which has built-in TWAP) depend on external oracle feeds for price data. Oracle manipulation is a universal attack vector.
cross_protocol_patterns.over_collateralization: Aave, Maker, and Morpho all enforce over-collateralization as a fundamental invariant. Lido uses a different model (staked ETH backing).
cross_protocol_patterns.access_control_models: Role-based access control is universal: Aave uses Access Control Manager, Lido uses granular roles, Maker uses hope/nope auth, Morpho uses curator/allocator/admin.
cross_protocol_patterns.pausability: Aave, Lido, and Maker all support emergency pause mechanisms. Uniswap V3 core pools are not pausable.
cross_protocol_patterns.upgrade_risk: Maker (immutable Vat but join contracts can change), Lido (V3 with new VaultHub surface), Aave (V3 to V4 migration with new Hub/Spoke model).
cross_protocol_patterns.mev_frontrunning: Lido explicitly mitigates rebase frontrunning. Uniswap documentation warns about sandwich attacks. Aave flash loans are an MEV tool.
cross_protocol_patterns.formal_verification: Morpho applies formal verification alongside traditional audits. Maker's Vat is described as immutable 'constitution'.
